title: Tongue Position Matters
description: Hindi has two sets of similar-sounding consonants that differ only in where your tongue touches inside your mouth. 'Dental' sounds are made with your tongue touching the back of your upper TEETH (like the Spanish 't'). 'Retroflex' sounds are made by curling your tongue BACK to touch the roof of your mouth. English doesn't have true retroflex sounds, so this will feel unusual at first — but it's one of the most distinctive features of Hindi pronunciation!
keyPoints: Dental (दन्त्य): tongue touches the back of the upper front teeth
Retroflex (मूर्धन्य): tongue curls back to touch the hard palate (roof of mouth)
Hindi has 5 dental-retroflex pairs: ट/त, ठ/थ, ड/द, ढ/ध, ण/न
Mixing them up can change word meanings: दाल (lentils) vs डाल (branch)

title: Dental vs Retroflex: The Tongue Rule
explanation: Feel the ridge behind your upper front teeth with your tongue. For DENTAL sounds (त, थ, द, ध, न), your tongue tip touches your TEETH or just behind them — very forward. For RETROFLEX sounds (ट, ठ, ड, ढ, ण), curl your tongue tip BACKWARD so the underside of your tongue touches the hard palate (the bumpy roof of your mouth). It feels like your tongue is doing a little flip!
simpleRule: Dental = tongue touches TEETH (त, थ, द, ध, न). Retroflex = tongue curls BACK to the roof (ट, ठ, ड, ढ, ण).

vietnamese: ṭa (uốn lưỡi, lưỡi cong ra sau)
ḍa (uốn lưỡi, lưỡi cong ra sau)
tha (đầu lưỡi chạm răng + bật hơi — KHÔNG phải 'th' tiếng Anh!)

translation_en: What does this word mean? (Hint: tongue position changes meaning!)
translation_vi: Từ này có nghĩa là gì? (Gợi ý: vị trí lưỡi thay đổi nghĩa!)
options_vi: Đậu lăng (दाल - đầu lưỡi chạm răng)

vietnamese: dha (đầu lưỡi chạm răng + bật hơi)
na (âm mũi đầu lưỡi, như 'n' tiếng Anh)

translation: Practice alternating: retroflex ट, dental त, retroflex ड, dental द, retroflex ठ, dental थ, retroflex ढ, dental ध

translation_vi: Từ này có nghĩa là gì?
options_vi: Đậu lăng (दाल - đầu lưỡi chạm răng)

title: Retroflex vs Dental — Key Takeaways
takeaways: Dental sounds (त, थ, द, ध, न): tongue touches the TEETH
Retroflex sounds (ट, ठ, ड, ढ, ण): tongue curls BACK to the roof of the mouth
Hindi has 5 pairs of dental-retroflex consonants
Mixing them up changes meaning: दाल (lentils) vs डाल (branch)
English 'th' is NOT the same as Hindi थ — don't confuse them!
Practice by alternating between pairs: ट/त, ड/द, etc.
